assertions.title: Auth file detection works cross-platform

steps.expected: Uses cursor-agent status, not file-based fallback
steps.observed: Uses `cursor-agent status` command, file-based fallback removed

steps.expected: Shows not_authenticated when cursor-agent is not logged in

steps.observed: cli-config.json contains only user settings (permissions, editor, model) - NO access token

evidence.cliConfig: Only contains: permissions, editor, model preferences - NO auth tokens

assertions.issues: The fix for VAL-AUTH-002 is correct - auth detection now properly uses `cursor-agent status`. However, the underlying issue remains: when cursor-agent IS authenticated, the tokens are stored internally by cursor-agent and not in ~/.cursor/ files. The fix correctly handles the NOT authenticated case. Cannot verify authenticated case without logged-in cursor-agent.

assertions.title: Auth token extraction from CLI config

steps.observed: Contains ONLY user settings: permissions, editor, model, network, sandbox, attribution - NO access token field

steps.action: Verify fix: file-based fallback removed from verifyCursorAuth()
steps.expected: No longer relies on file existence for auth
steps.observed: verifyCursorAuth() now only calls `cursor-agent status`, no file-based fallback

evidence.network: cursor-agent stores tokens internally, not in ~/.cursor/

assertions.issues: VAL-AUTH-003 CANNOT be satisfied by design. cursor-agent does not store access tokens in ~/.cursor/cli-config.json or any other file in ~/.cursor/. Tokens are stored internally by cursor-agent's own mechanism. The fix correctly removes the incorrect file-based fallback. The assertion itself may need to be marked as non-testable or reworded to reflect the actual architecture.

assertions.title: Proxy handles missing auth gracefully

steps.observed: Model validation happens before auth check (fix for VAL-ERROR-003)

steps.expected: Returns 400 for unknown model before auth check
steps.observed: Returns 400 with model_not_found - auth not reached

evidence.issue: With current validation order, testing 401 for missing auth requires sending a request with a KNOWN model while unauthenticated. Without authenticated cursor-agent, cannot determine which models are valid.

assertions.issues: Cannot test 401 response without a known-valid model. The fix for VAL-ERROR-003 changed validation order to check model before auth, which means unknown models return 400 before auth is even checked. To properly test VAL-PROXY-005, need an authenticated cursor-agent to know which models are valid, then test with invalid auth.

assertions.title: Quota exceeded is detected
assertions.issues: Cannot test without triggering real API usage limits. Would require actual Cursor API quota consumption.

blockers.description: cursor-agent is not logged in, cannot test authenticated flows
cursor-agent stores tokens internally, not in accessible files
Cannot trigger real API quota errors without consuming actual API limits

summary: Verified fixes from round 2. VAL-ERROR-003 is now FIXED (returns 400 for unknown model). VAL-AUTH-002/003 fixes are correct - auth detection now uses `cursor-agent status` properly, but underlying architectural limitation remains (cursor-agent tokens not in files). VAL-PROXY-005 and VAL-ERROR-002 remain blocked due to environment constraints.
